summary:
There is no supporting evidence in the provided materials regarding specific timing rules for packed RBC transfusions—such as starting or finishing within certain hours. Consequently, all proposed options are unsubstantiated; however, selecting “wait until patient is stable” may appear reasonable under misinterpretations of urgency, though it lacks basis in the actual evidence.  

(Note: This summary focuses purely on clinically relevant facts derived directly from the agent’s analysis.) 

(End of Summary)
